articles.title: Anatomy of the TanStack npm Compromise
articles.description: A new strain of the Shai-Hulud worm pushed malicious versions of TanStack packages to npm yesterday (containing a tripwire that would delete files if it detected token revocation), though it hit ~170 other packages too. Maintainer credentials weren’t stolen, with the attack instead chaining pull_request_target abuse, cache poisoning, and OIDC token theft from CI memory.
articles.author: Tanner Linsley

articles.title: Announcing Rolldown 1.0: The High Performance JS Bundler
articles.description: The Rust-based bundler built as the backbone for Vite 8 reaches a stable v1.0. You get huge performance gains, but with Rollup plugin API compatibility: it's 10–30x faster than Rollup, with early adopters reporting big drops in build time.
articles.author: The VoidZero Team

articles.title: 33 JavaScript Concepts
articles.description: What began life as a Medium article and turned into a popular GitHub repo is now a full site covering a wide array of JavaScript concepts, even going beyond the 33.
articles.author: Leonardo Maldonado

articles.title: 9 Times the Web Platform Was Influenced by JavaScript Libraries
articles.description: How various libraries like Lodash, Dojo and jQuery often did the “R&D work in production” for various features that eventually ended up in browser APIs.
articles.author: Jad Joubran

articles.title: From React to Web Components: A Migration That Saved 100 KB
articles.description: “How I migrated a site from React to native Web Components, why that worked better than I expected, and how the patterns I used along the way grew into a small library called nanotags.”
articles.author: Pavel Grinchenko (Evil Martians)

articles.title: Why Migrate to Valibot?
articles.description: Valibot is a light, modular TypeScript schema validation library and an alternative to the likes of Zod. v1.4.0 just dropped, too.
articles.author: Fabian Hiller

articles.title: zero-native: Build Desktop Apps with Zig + WebView
articles.description: Vercel Labs’ entry into the Neutralinojs/Electron/Tauri space for building native HTML+JS desktop apps atop a Zig core and the system WebView or Chromium. There are examples covering how to build vanilla, React, Svelte, and Vue apps on it. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Vercel

articles.title: Wakaru: Pull Apart Minified JavaScript Bundles
articles.description: A tool you can feed minified bundled code and get readable modules back, whether for recovering code, reverse-engineering, or security auditing. You can try it online here.
articles.author: Pionxzh

articles.title: BlueJS: Compile JavaScript to Tiny Binaries
articles.description: An ahead-of-time compiler for JavaScript with QuickJS optionally embedded for dynamic features and package support. While closed source, the raw numbers are compelling (~5ms startup; 3.8MB peak memory use, and a GUI app in a 1.2MB binary).
articles.author: BlueJS

articles.description: The notes from Node.js's recent collaboration summit make for good reading if you want to see what the Node team is working on right now.
Regarding Bun's experimental port to Rust (from Zig), Jarred Sumner says the now-960k LOC rewrite passes 99.8% of Bun's pre-existing test suite.
🤖 A developer has launched a challenge to port NetHack to JavaScript. Can LLMs port the complex roguelike game perfectly? So far, no, with the leaderboard showing a transpiled version coming closest.
Did you know Firefox has a $$$ helper for querying through the Shadow DOM?
Jest 30.4.0 – A strong release for the popular JavaScript testing framework as it improves ESM, Temporal and React 19 support.

Node 26.1 (Current) – Last week's big Node 26 release may have had the Temporal API, but 26.1 adds (experimental) official FFI support. I did a writeup of what this means at the end of last week's Node Weekly.
Electron 42 – Updates to Chromium 148/Node 24.15/V8 14.8 and no longer downloads the Electron binary in a postinstall script for added security.
Capacitor 9.0 Alpha 1, Playwright 1.60.0, Mantine 9.2

articles.title: Safari 26.5 Released: Here's the WebKit Features
articles.description: Beyond the usual polish, 26.5 includes support for the :open pseudo-class, improvements to the CSS random() function, anchor positioning fixes, support for color-interpolation in SVG gradients, and Origin API support.
articles.author: Jen Simmons

articles.title: Meet Your Users Where They Are with Obs.js
articles.description: Obs.js is a tiny inline script that reads browser signals (latency, bandwidth, Data Saver, battery, CPU, memory) and exposes them as classes on <html> (and via window.obs). You can use these to serve smaller images on weak networks, skip animations on low battery, and adapt to your users’ real-world conditions.
articles.author: Harry Roberts

articles.title: Install Web Apps with the New <install> Element
articles.description: A new HTML element is available for testing in Edge and Chrome (behind a flag) that renders a trusted 'install' button for web apps with no JS needed. This article digs into the details of how it works and how it compares to the Web Install API.
articles.author: Patrick Brosset

articles.title: Controlling the Speed of Infinite Animations
articles.description: Learn how to control the speed of infinite CSS animations on user interaction, allowing for accelerating, slowing down, stopping, or reversing animations with a CSS variable.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: Soon We Can Finally Banish JavaScript to the ShadowRealm
articles.description: Mat takes us through the in-progress TC39 proposal for running JavaScript in an isolated ‘pseudo-realm’ with its own globals and intrinsics — handy for third-party code and anything you want to keep away from your global scope.
articles.author: Mat Marquis

articles.title: Better Browser Caching with No-Vary-Search
articles.description: No-Vary-Search is an HTTP response header that tells caches which URL parameters can be ignored when matching requests to cached responses. While support is limited in browsers, for now, it's a general HTTP caching extension, so CDNs and shared caches can benefit too.
articles.author: Harry Roberts

articles.title: Six Million Selections Later: How the DMA Is Giving People Browser Choice
articles.description: Two years on, the EU’s Digital Markets Act has been beneficial for Mozilla: “Firefox is selected through a DMA browser choice screen every 10 seconds. That adds up to more than six million Firefox selections”.
articles.author: Gemma Petrie and Tasos Stampelos (Mozilla)

articles.title: Testing Vue Components in the Browser
articles.description: Julia sets up integration tests for her components that run entirely in the browser. She shares the issues she encountered while maintaining her approach of writing JavaScript for the frontend without using Node or server-side tooling.
articles.author: Julia Evans

articles.title: wf: A CLI Tool for Looking Up Web Platform Features
articles.description: A CLI tool for looking up web platform feature support, bugs, and docs, from Patrick Brosset of the Microsoft Edge team. You can install and run it as wf or you can just run it with npx like so: npx web-features-cli "fetch" .. The data is fetched live, too, so it’s always up to date.
articles.author: Patrick Brosset (Microsoft)

articles.title: Anime.js 4.4: The Flexible Animation Engine for HTML Elements
articles.description: At ten years old, the ‘animate anything from JavaScript’ library continues to get even better with a new scrambleText effect and auto-grid layout mode for stagger grids. The docs for Anime are truly top-tier.
articles.author: Julian Garnier

articles.title: Cascade: A Set of SVG & React Icons for CSS Properties
articles.description: A niche set of 97 icons specifically for representing CSS properties (e.g. a square for display: block, a two-column box for display: flex, and so on). Ideal if you're building design tools. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Andrew Flett

articles.title: visual-explainer: An Agent Skill That Turns Complex Terminal Output Into Styled HTML Pages
articles.description: The idea here is to enable your agent to produce a more readable output than ASCII art or box diagrams, especially for more complex concepts like system architecture, diff reviews, and so on.
articles.author: Nico Bailon

articles.title: Find Font: A Web Tool to Browse, Compare & Download Fonts
articles.description: Includes a live comparison tool and an AI-powered font-pairing tool, with 5000+ free curated fonts to select from, in 50+ categories. You can view trending fonts, new additions, and compare up to 10 fonts on the same page.
articles.author: Kaan Asici
